title: [Feature]: Evaluate an Azure Event Hubs sink
problem: Oracle GoldenGate lists Azure Event Hubs as a source and target. nats-sinks does not yet have an Azure streaming handoff sink.
proposal: Research an Azure Event Hubs sink using the Azure SDK with explicit namespace/event-hub validation, managed identity support, deterministic partition-key options, bounded batch publishing, partial-failure handling, and payload encryption compatibility.
users: Azure platform teams and cross-cloud operators who need NATS JetStream events delivered to Event Hubs for analytics, Functions, or downstream processing.
delivery_semantics: ACK-gated success requires confirmed Event Hubs batch send for every required message. Partial send failures must prevent ACK for failed records.
security: Prefer managed identity or workload identity. Connection strings, SAS tokens, namespace names, payloads, and partition keys must be redacted by default.
acceptance: Research documents Event Hubs batch size, partitioning, and retry behavior.
The design handles partial failures and duplicate delivery.
Unit tests use Azure SDK fakes and no network calls.
Documentation covers least privilege and operational limitations.
tests: Add unit tests for partition strategy, batch construction, error translation, and ACK ordering. Gate live Azure tests behind explicit environment variables.
documentation: Add Azure Event Hubs sink documentation if implemented and update roadmap and security guidance.
closeout: Close after the research or implementation release is published.
